edges.explanation: 2Y yield tracks expected short-term rates almost immediately. Fed hikes → market prices higher rates for next 2 years → 2Y rises.
10Y rises with hikes but less reliably — it also reflects long-run growth and inflation expectations beyond Fed control.
Higher US rates attract foreign capital into US assets, increasing demand for USD.
Stocks reprice instantly on FOMC announcements — sentiment and forward guidance hit markets within seconds.
Higher 10Y = higher discount rate. Stock price = Earnings / Discount Rate, so rising 10Y compresses valuations.
30Y mortgage rates are benchmarked directly to the 10Y Treasury. They move in lockstep with a short lag.
Stronger dollar hurts S&P 500 multinationals — foreign revenues translate back to fewer dollars.
